title: picris comosa subsp. lusitanica
intro: Picris comosa subsp. lusitanica, also known as Lusitanian Hawk's Beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to Portugal and western Spain. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Picris comosa subsp. lusitanica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, it is typically used as a tincture or tea, and is also incorporated into salves and ointments for topical application.
